heading: Rights-of-way, easements, etc.; acquisition by local authorities; maintenance and operation; protection of United States from liability for damages; requisites to run-off and water-flow retardation and soil erosion prevention assistance
label: 701c Rights-of-way, easements, etc.; acquisition by local authorities; maintenance and operation; protection of United States from liability for damages; requisites to run-off and water-flow retardation and soil erosion prevention assistance
notes: References in TextHerein, referred to in text, means , , popularly known as the Flood Control Act of , which to the extent classified to the Code enacted sections 701a, 701b, 701c, 701d to 701f, and 701h of this title. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Tables. Portions of section 5 of act , enumerating certain dams to be constructed, were not classified to the Code.act June 22, 1936, ch. 68849 Stat. 1570June 22, 1936June 22, 1936

Change of NameDepartment of War designated Department of the Army and title of Secretary of War changed to Secretary of the Army by section 205(a) of , . Section 205(a) of act , was repealed by section 53 of , . Section 1 of act , enacted “Title 10, Armed Forces” which in sections 3010 to 3013 continued Department of the Army under administrative supervision of Secretary of the Army.act July 26, 1947, ch. 343, title II61 Stat. 501act Aug. 10, 1956, ch. 104170A Stat. 641July 26, 1947Aug. 10, 1956

Applicability of Section to Flood Control Works Authorized by Flood Control Acts, , , provided that:Pub. L. 90–483, title II, § 20182 Stat. 739Aug. 13, 1968“Section 3 of the Act approved (Public Law Numbered 738, Seventy-fourth Congress) [this section], as amended by section 2 of the Act approved (Public Law Numbered 761, Seventy-fifth Congress) [], shall apply to all works authorized in this title except that for any channel improvement or channel rectification project, provisions (a), (b), and (c) of section 3 of said Act of [this section], shall apply thereto, except as otherwise provided by law. The authorization for any flood control project herein authorized by this Act [] requiring local cooperation shall expire five years from the date on which local interests are notified in writing by the Secretary of the Army or his designee of the requirements of local cooperation, unless said interests shall within said time furnish assurances satisfactory to the Secretary of the Army that the required cooperation will be furnished.”June 22, 1936June 28, 1938June 22, 1936section 701c–1 of this titlePub. L. 90–483Similar provisions were contained in the following prior acts:, , .Nov. 7, 1966Pub. L. 89–789, title II, § 20180 Stat. 1418, , .Oct. 27, 1965Pub. Application of Section, , provided that this section, as theretofore amended and therein further modified, should apply to all flood control projects, except as otherwise specifically provided by law. For modification of this section by that act, see .Act June 28, 1938, ch. 795, § 252 Stat. 1215section 701c–1 of this title

children.text: The enactment and reasonable safeguards for the enforcement of State and local laws imposing suitable permanent restrictions on the use of such lands and otherwise providing for run-off and waterflow retardation and soil-erosion prevention;
Agreements or covenants as to the permanent use of such lands; and
Contributions in money, services, materials, or otherwise to any operations conferring such benefits.
